overview.tagline: A proud Meadowlands township with strong Italian roots, a lively downtown, and Meadowlands access
overview.description: Lyndhurst is a densely populated township in southeastern Bergen County, known for its vibrant Italian-American heritage, bustling Ridge Road commercial district, and proximity to the Meadowlands Sports Complex. The township offers an energetic, community-oriented atmosphere with parades, festivals, and a strong sense of local pride. Its location at the intersection of major highways provides excellent transportation access, while the nearby Meadowlands offers both entertainment and natural areas including the DeKorte Park nature center.
highlights: Vibrant Ridge Road commercial district with Italian delis, bakeries, and restaurants
Strong Italian-American heritage and community traditions
Adjacent to MetLife Stadium, American Dream, and Meadowlands Sports Complex
Meadowlands Environment Center at DeKorte Park nearby
NJ Transit train station on the Meadowlands line

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Lyndhurst's housing stock includes single-family homes, two-family properties, condominiums, and some newer townhouse developments.
realEstate.recentTrends: Lyndhurst's housing market has seen steady appreciation, driven by its relative affordability, transit access, and proximity to NYC. New construction near the Meadowlands has added townhouse and condominium inventory. The Ridge Road area is popular for its walkability.
neighborhoodNotes: Ridge Road area — Walking distance to shops, restaurants, and the commercial center
Valley Brook Avenue — Mix of residential homes near schools and parks
Kingsland area — Near the train station and Meadowlands attractions
North end — Quieter residential streets bordering Rutherford

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Township of Lyndhurst.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment
Holiday schedule posted on township website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected weekly by the Township.
details: Commingled recyclables collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on the same schedule
Recycling bins available through the DPW
Yard waste collected seasonally
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water and sewer service is managed by the Township of Lyndhurst.
details: Water and sewer billing handled by the Township
Quarterly billing for water and sewer service
Water quality reports published annually
Sewer service maintained by the Township DPW
Emergency repairs available 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board
Permit applications available at Township Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Township manages on-street parking and municipal lots, including commuter parking.
details: Metered parking along Ridge Road in the commercial district
Municipal parking lots available near downtown
Commuter parking at the Lyndhurst train station
Overnight parking restrictions on township streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced

additionalServices.title: Lyndhurst Free Public Library
additionalServices.description: Public library serving township residents with books, programs, and digital resources.
details: Located at 355 Valley Brook Avenue
Children's programs, teen events, and adult programs
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Free Wi-Fi and computer access

additionalServices.title: Parks & Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues and summer programs
Senior citizen programs at the senior center
Community events and festivals
Township parks and athletic facilities

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Lyndhurst enforces noise regulations to protect residential quality of life.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 8:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or holidays
Power equipment restricted to daytime hours
Excessive noise from gatherings subject to police enforcement

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained at a reasonable height
Sidewalk snow removal required within 24 hours
Properties must be free of debris and refuse
Abandoned vehicles must be removed
Code enforcement actively monitors property conditions

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Lyndhurst requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Nuisance barking subject to enforcement
No exotic animals permitted

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is regulated throughout the township including the busy Ridge Road corridor.
keyRules: Metered parking along Ridge Road with time limits
Overnight parking restricted on many township streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Commercial vehicles prohibited on residential streets overnight
Handicapped parking violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and comply with housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Township
Certificate of Habitability required
Landlords must maintain properties to code
Inspections at change of tenancy
Multi-family dwellings subject to additional requirements

other.title: Outdoor Dining
other.summary: Restaurants along Ridge Road may apply for outdoor dining permits.
keyRules: Outdoor dining permits issued by the Township
Minimum sidewalk clearance must be maintained
Furniture must be secured or removed nightly
Compliance with health department regulations required

publicSchools.name: Lyndhurst High School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive high school with strong athletics and a variety of academic and extracurricular programs

publicSchools.name: Lyndhurst Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Middle school serving all township students in grades 6-8

publicSchools.name: Columbus Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the eastern section of the township

publicSchools.name: Franklin Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school centrally located in the township

publicSchools.name: Jefferson Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the northern portion of the township

publicSchools.name: Roosevelt Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the Kingsland area

publicSchools.name: Washington Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the western part of the township

nearbyHigherEd: New Jersey Institute of Technology (Newark) — 20 minutes

whyMoveHere: Affordable Bergen County location with character and community pride
Vibrant Ridge Road with Italian delis, bakeries, and restaurants
NJ Transit Meadowlands line station for events and connections
Adjacent to MetLife Stadium, American Dream, and Meadowlands entertainment
Strong community traditions with parades, festivals, and civic organizations
Excellent highway access to Routes 3, 17, and the NJ Turnpike

commuteToNYC.duration: 30-50 minutes to Port Authority Bus Terminal
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus routes provide direct service to Manhattan. The Lyndhurst train station on the Meadowlands line operates limited service primarily for events at MetLife Stadium. Secaucus Junction is a short drive for NJ Transit rail connections.

costOfLiving.summary: Lyndhurst offers moderate living costs for Bergen County, with affordable housing and a range of budget-friendly restaurants and services.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 10% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Affordable housing by Bergen County standards
Vibrant commercial district with authentic Italian food
Strong community identity and active civic life
Close to Meadowlands entertainment and sports
cons: Meadowlands event traffic can impact local roads
Flood-prone areas near the Hackensack River and Meadowlands
No regular commuter train service — reliant on buses
Industrial areas near the Meadowlands corridor
Property taxes relatively high for the school ratings

restaurants.name: Mazur's Bakery
restaurants.description: Legendary local bakery famous for its cream puffs, cakes, pastries, and breads. A Lyndhurst institution since 1939.

restaurants.name: Whiskey Barrel
restaurants.description: Modern American restaurant and bar offering craft cocktails, elevated comfort food, and a lively atmosphere on Ridge Road.

restaurants.name: San Carlo Restaurant
restaurants.description: Classic Italian restaurant offering traditional pasta dishes, veal, and seafood in an old-school dining room.

restaurants.name: Mignon Steakhouse
restaurants.description: Upscale steakhouse offering prime cuts, seafood, and an extensive wine list in a refined setting.

restaurants.name: Brownstone Pancake Factory
restaurants.description: Popular brunch destination known for creative pancakes, stuffed French toast, and hearty breakfast platters.

parks.name: Town Hall Park
parks.description: Central township park with a gazebo, walking paths, playground, and venue for community events and concerts.

parks.name: Riverside County Park
parks.description: Bergen County park along the Passaic River with walking paths, athletic fields, and scenic waterfront views.

parks.name: DeKorte Park (Meadowlands Environment Center)
parks.description: Nature center and park in the Meadowlands featuring walking trails, bird observation areas, and environmental education programs. A surprising natural oasis near the sports complex.

events.name: Lyndhurst Street Fair
events.description: Annual street fair along Ridge Road with vendors, food, rides, live entertainment, and community activities.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: One of Bergen County's largest Memorial Day parades, marching through the township with bands, veterans, and civic organizations.

events.name: Lyndhurst Holiday Parade
events.description: Annual holiday parade with floats, marching bands, and a visit from Santa Claus, a beloved community tradition.

events.name: Summer Concerts in the Park
events.description: Free outdoor concerts held in Town Hall Park throughout the summer months.

attractions.name: Ridge Road Shopping District
attractions.description: Lyndhurst's bustling main street lined with Italian delis, bakeries, restaurants, and shops. The cultural and commercial heart of the township.

attractions.name: Meadowlands Environment Center at DeKorte Park
attractions.description: Environmental education center with exhibits on the Meadowlands ecosystem, walking trails through marshland, and birding opportunities.

attractions.name: MetLife Stadium & American Dream
attractions.description: World-class sports and entertainment venues adjacent to Lyndhurst, hosting NFL games, concerts, and a massive indoor entertainment complex.

shoppingAreas: Ridge Road — Main commercial district with shops, delis, and restaurants
Valley Brook Avenue — Additional shops and services

government.governingBody: Board of Commissioners (Commissioner form of government)

council.name: Thomas DiMaggio
council.title: Commissioner of Parks and Public Property

government.meetingSchedule: Board of Commissioners meets the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Town Hall, 367 Valley Brook Avenue, Lyndhurst, NJ 07071

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
